title: Smoky Roasted Saudi-Arabian Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful dish from Saudi Arabia, featuring lamb, dates, and allspice, perfect for a romantic dinner. This recipe is inspired by Ayurvedic cooking principles, aiming to balance the Rajasic energies of the dish.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to high heat. Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and ground allspice. Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the lamb until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes per side.
Transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside. Add the sliced onions to the pot and cook until they start to caramelize, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the pitted dates to the pot and cook for 2 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cinnamon stick to the pot and stir to combine.
Return the lamb to the pot and cover with a lid. Transfer the pot to the preheated broiler and cook for 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
Remove the pot from the broiler and let it cool slightly. Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
